input:
He moved fast and changed his route, taking the coastal way, in the hope of avoiding the Muslims. A Dream Warning of Immediate Danger Three days before the arrival of Ḍamḍam in Makkah, ʿĀtikah bint ʿAbd al-Muṭṭalib, the Prophet’s paternal aunt who still lived in Makkah, had a significant dream. She sent for her brother al-ʿAbbās and told him that in her dream she saw a man riding a camel coming to a place in Makkah called al-Abṭaḥ, where he stood and shouted: “Rise, you people, and move to your deaths in three days’ time.” People gathered round him and followed him as he moved towards the mosque, where he repeated his warning.
Source: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 17: Badr: The First Major Battle

output.source_reference: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 17: Badr: The First Major Battle

characters.name: ʿĀtikah bint ʿAbd al-Muṭṭalib
characters.appearance: Middle-aged, medium build, dignified posture. Wearing a loose-fitting black thobe, wrapped in a simple white headscarf, dark leather sandals. Soft, gentle features, slight smile.

characters.name: al-ʿAbbās ibn ʿAbd al-Muṭṭalib
characters.appearance: Middle-aged, strong build, dignified posture. Wearing a simple off-white thobe, white turban, dark leather sandals. Strong features, kind eyes.

characters.name: Unknown
characters.appearance: Tall, dark figure, riding a camel, wearing a black cloak, shrouded in shadows.

output.moral_lesson: Allah can warn us of impending dangers through various means, including dreams.
output.narrative_text: Three days before Ḍamḍam's arrival, ʿĀtikah bint ʿAbd al-Muṭṭalib had a dream. She saw a man warning people of their deaths in three days' time. He stood in al-Abṭaḥ, shouting to the people to rise and move towards their deaths.

compliance.notes: No prophets or idols shown. Aunt Atikah and Abbas shown with full faces. The camel rider is shown as a silhouette, avoiding any potential depiction of the Prophet.

output.image_prompt: Watercolor illustration. Nighttime scene in Makkah. A middle-aged woman, ʿĀtikah, sits on a woven mat, wearing a loose-fitting black thobe and simple white headscarf, looking concerned. Her brother al-ʿAbbās stands beside her, wearing a simple off-white thobe and white turban. In the background, a tall dark figure, riding a camel, is seen as a silhouette, wearing a black cloak. The scene is set against a dark blue night sky with stars. Medium shot, eye level. Muted colors. No modern objects.
